title: Masonry Reel Shot-Sheet: Your Creative Is the Shelf
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel. ~29.5 seconds total. Featured brand: Poppi (real D2C prebiotic soda). Inspired by the @mobileeditingclub / lovart.ai reel.
concept: For a D2C soda brand, the creative is the shelf. Masonry turns one can shot into a full campaign: ads, lifestyle scenes, creator-style clips, even a flavor launch, with no studio, no shoot, no waiting. Same beat structure and length as the inspiration reel, with fully original copy.
audio: Bright, upbeat trending-style track with a clear rhythmic pulse to cut on. The on-screen text carries the message, so this reel works with OR without a spoken voice-over. If the inspiration is audio-only (no person speaking), leave each beat's Voice-over blank and let the on-screen text plus this track and the visuals do the talking; sync each cut to the beat of the track.

beats.visual: Open on a creator talking straight to camera, holding a cold Poppi can up near their face. Warm, hand-held, strong eye contact so it reads like a real person stopping your scroll, not an ad.

beats.vo: Which can would you actually grab?
beats.visual: Hard cut to a dull, flat product photo of a soda can on a plain counter, phone-snapshot lighting, slightly lifeless. It should look ordinary, so the next beat lands.
Snap to a vibrant editorial Poppi can on a bold color-blocked set, beaded condensation, a splash frozen mid-air, punchy golden light. Same product, completely different pull.

beats.vo: Because for a soda brand fighting for attention,
beats.visual: Fast scroll through a phone showing feed ads and a shelf of competitor cans, as if the viewer is deciding what to grab. Keep the motion quick.
A clean grid of Poppi ad creatives animates into place: a feed ad, a story ad, a banner, all matching in tone. The point landing: online, your imagery is the shelf.

beats.vo: And great product visuals used to mean a studio, a set, a photographer,
beats.ost: great visuals used to need a STUDIO
beats.visual: Behind-the-scenes beverage-shoot b-roll: lights, a splash rig, someone styling cans on a set. Intentionally busy and effortful to signal expensive and slow.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration 8s to 9s, the studio and process beat.

beats.vo: and a budget a young brand rarely has.
beats.ost: and a budget brands rarely have
beats.visual: Cut to a founder at a laptop looking a little stuck, scrolling an empty content calendar. Relatable small-team reality.
Hard cut to black for a beat, then the Masonry logo snaps in. The pattern interrupt right before the solution.

beats.vo: With Masonry, one can shot becomes
beats.visual: A single clean Poppi can sits centered on screen and holds for a moment, so it reads as the one input that is about to multiply.
The single can fans out into three polished ad layouts, each carrying Poppi branding and flavor color.
Quick two-beat: the can on a sunny picnic blanket, then sweating on a poolside ledge.
A vertical, casual creator-cracks-the-can-and-sips clip that looks like authentic user content, not a polished ad.
A full campaign grid assembles: hero can, ads, story frames, an email banner, all visually cohesive.

beats.vo: Just drop in your can and describe the vibe,
beats.visual: Screen recording of the Masonry interface: the user drags the can image in and types a short prompt describing the look they want.

beats.vo: and Masonry generates it, on-brand, in seconds.
beats.visual: Screen recording continues: the generated result reveals with Poppi colors, logo and flavor cues already intact, no manual design step in between.

beats.vo: Want it on a beach? A hand holding it? A new flavor color?
beats.ost: want it on a BEACH? a new flavor?
beats.visual: Three quick variation thumbnails flicker past, each a different mood and flavor of the same can.

beats.vo: Just tell it. It reshoots in seconds.
beats.visual: Screen recording: the user edits the prompt and a new on-brand can image swaps in live, showing how fast the iteration is.

beats.vo: That is a launch campaign. No studio. No waiting.
beats.visual: Pull back to a final hero grid of all the Poppi assets together: polished, consistent, clearly a complete set.

beats.vo: This is how D2C brands create in 2026. Comment CREATE and we will send you the link.
beats.visual: End card: Masonry logo on a clean on-brand background with the Comment CREATE prompt. Hold to the end.

notes: Demo brand is Poppi, a realistic stand-in for a D2C beverage client (swap in the actual client brand when producing). All product, lifestyle and UI footage would be generated or assembled with Masonry; no live media has been generated yet (generation is gated on separate approval). Keep total runtime within 29 to 30 seconds to match the reference.
